skill.name: vexor
skill.description: Vector-powered CLI guidance for semantic file search with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.summary: Use Vexor patterns to support semantic file search in code and document repositories.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two URL findings and two weak cryptographic algorithm findings. The URLs are documentation links to the source repository, and the weak crypto matches appear in prose rather than executable code. No evidence found of prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution, or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Repository Links
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md lines 4 and 22. Both URLs identify the source repository and do not perform runtime network access, credential transfer, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only markdown metadata and prose. The URLs are repository references, not executable network calls.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 20. These lines describe vector-powered semantic file search and do not contain cryptographic code, hash functions, encryption calls, or implementation instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is descriptive markdown, and no executable weak cryptography usage is present. Confidence is high because the skill has only one short markdown file.

content.value_statement: Finding relevant files by exact keyword can miss important context. Vexor provides guidance for using vector-powered semantic file search with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Describes when to use vector-powered semantic file search.
Frames Vexor as a CLI-based workflow for finding relevant files.
Supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code marketplace compatibility.
Links users to the upstream Vexor source repository.
Provides lightweight guidance without requiring bundled scripts.
limitations: The skill file does not include executable CLI code.
Users must consult the source repository for installation details.
The skill does not define indexing settings or model choices.
The audit reviewed only the provided SKILL.md file.

use_cases.title: Find Related Code Faster
use_cases.description: Use semantic search guidance to locate files related by meaning, not only by exact words.

use_cases.title: Explore Unknown Repositories
use_cases.description: Use Vexor concepts to identify relevant files before editing unfamiliar code.

use_cases.title: Improve Code Review Preparation
use_cases.description: Search for related implementation areas before reviewing a change or planning a fix.

prompt_templates.title: Find Files by Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Vexor-style semantic file search to find files related to: [topic]. Summarize why each result may be relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Semantic Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for files related to [feature or bug]. Group results by likely responsibility and explain which files to inspect first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Triage for a focused code task

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Edit Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use semantic file search concepts to identify files involved in [change request]. Create an edit plan with risks and verification steps.

prompt_templates.title: Map Cross-Cutting Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Find files semantically related to [workflow]. Separate direct implementation, tests, configuration, and documentation. Note missing evidence clearly.

output_examples.input: Find files related to authentication token refresh.
output_examples.output: A concise list of likely authentication, session, and API client files, with a short relevance note for each file.

output_examples.input: Identify files related to search indexing.
output_examples.output: Grouped results covering index creation, query handling, configuration, and tests, followed by suggested inspection order.
A task-focused map of validation files, related tests, and potential integration points to review before editing.

best_practices: Start with a clear concept or workflow instead of a broad keyword.
Verify semantic search results by reading the selected files.
Record why each file is relevant before making changes.
anti_patterns: Do not assume semantic matches are complete without checking tests and configuration.
Do not edit files based only on search ranking.
Do not use repository links as proof that installed code is safe.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users apply Vexor-style semantic file search concepts while working with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains only markdown guidance and repository links.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low instead of safe?
faq.answer: The file includes external repository links, so a network risk indicator is disclosed even though no runtime network call exists.

faq.question: Were the weak cryptography findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The flagged lines are descriptive text and do not contain cryptographic implementation.

faq.question: Can this replace reading source files?
faq.answer: No. Semantic search can suggest relevant files, but users still need to inspect source content.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers and AI coding assistant users who need to find relevant files in larger repositories.
